head.hi: The Minister in Switzerland (Wilson) to the Secretary of State

div.p: 73. Assembly passed resolutionForeign Relations, Japan, 1931–1941, vol. i, p. 210. unchanged from text reported in Gilbert’s 116, March 11, 2 p.m.Not printed.

Drummond handed me at once letter of which text follows:
“I beg to enclose herewith for the information of your Government the text of a resolution relative to the Sino-Japanese dispute which was adopted this afternoon by the Assembly of the League of Nations.
In view of the collaboration which has on several occasions in the course of recent events been effected between the Government of the United States and the governments of other powers having special interests in the Shanghai Settlement, perhaps you will allow me to draw your particular attention to part II of the Assembly resolution in which the Assembly addresses a request to the powers members of the League of Nations which have such special interests.”

In giving me this letter he explained that it seemed more tactful not to ask for an answer from the American Government but merely to draw attention to part II of the resolution, but that of course if you cared to give an answer it would complete the record of cooperation which has been maintained throughout this negotiation.
